message:
fix(cloudflare): percent-encode KV keys in URL paths and filter bogus HTTP status codes

KV key encoding: Rename sanitize_kv_key to encode_kv_key and apply
percent_encoding::utf8_percent_encode(NON_ALPHANUMERIC) to the key
before interpolating it into the URL path. KV keys can contain slashes
and special characters in their logical names, but these must be
percent-encoded so the HTTP server treats them as a single path segment.
Without this, a key like "config/prod" would create an incorrect URL
with an extra path component.

Error mapping: Only include status_code in FcpError::External when the
Cloudflare API error code is in the 100-599 HTTP status range. Cloudflare
API error codes like 6003 or 10000 are not HTTP status codes and should
not be propagated as such, since downstream retry logic keys on status
code ranges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -82,7 +82,11 @@ impl CloudflareError {
             Self::Api { code, message } => FcpError::External {
                 service: "cloudflare".into(),
                 message: format!("Cloudflare API error {code}: {message}"),
-                status_code: u16::try_from(*code).ok(),
+                // Only include status_code if it looks like a valid HTTP status (100-599).
+                // Cloudflare API error codes (e.g. 6003, 10000) are NOT HTTP status codes.
+                status_code: u16::try_from(*code)
+                    .ok()
+                    .filter(|&c| (100..600).contains(&c)),
                 retryable: self.is_retryable(),
                 retry_after: self.retry_after(),
             },
